headline: Peloton Stock Is Surging Like It’s 2020. Can the Momentum Continue?
summary: Peloton Interactive  stock was soaring on Thursday after fiscal third-quarter financial results reinvigorated investor confidence that the at-home fitness company could score a much needed comeback.  Peloton reported adjusted earnings of six cents a share, which missed analyst estimates of 8 cents a share, according to  FactSet  Revenue for the quarter of $630.9 million beat analyst estimates of $618.3 million.  In the same period last year, Peloton reported an adjusted loss of 12 cents a share on revenue of $624 million.
